items.title: British royal family’s dilemma over Andrew’s daughters

items.title: China moves to expand role in Iran war diplomacy; U.S. seems sceptical
items.summary: Putting forward a five-point proposal with Pakistan, rallying support from Gulf nations and opposing a UN move over the Strait of Hormuz, China seeks a more active role in ending the conflict in West Asia; however, Washington appears less enthusiastic at the prospect of Beijing’s mediation

items.title: Iran-Israel war updates: Iranian minister dismisses Trump's threat, calls him 'unstable, delusional figure'

items.summary: Airstrikes also targeted the Sharif University of Technology in Tehran; Israel’s army said it had completed a wave of strikes against targets in Tehran

items.title: Aid groups warn war in West Asia is hindering food, medicine from reaching millions
items.summary: Key pathways such as the Strait of Hormuz have been effectively shuttered and routes from strategic hubs such as Dubai, Doha and Abu Dhabi have also been impacted; transport costs have spiked with higher fuel and insurance rates, meaning less supplies can be delivered with the same amount of money.

items.title: Bangladesh launches emergency vaccination campaign as measles outbreak spreads

items.title: AI is rewiring the world's most prolific film industry in India
items.summary: Studios in India are responding by deploying AI at a scale unseen elsewhere: creating full-fledged AI-generated films; using AI dubbing to release movies in numerous languages; and recutting endings of older titles to eke out additional sales

items.title: Trump appears to extend Iran deadline in cryptic post
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump, who has held no public events since an address to the nation on Wednesday (April 1, 2026), seemed to confirm the new timing in an interview with The Wall Street Journal

items.title: Taiwan opposition leader to make ‘peace’ visit to China, first in 10 years

items.summary: Visit likely on April 7 and 8; first FM visit since fall of Hasina regime; newly elected BNP indicates more willingness to negotiate Ganga water sharing treaty renewal, compared to the interim government

items.title: Iran military says U.S. rescue operation used abandoned airport in southern Isfahan
items.summary: "The so-called U.S. military rescue operation, planned as a deception and escape mission at an abandoned airport in southern Isfahan under the pretext of recovering the pilot of a downed aircraft, was completely foiled," said Ebrahim Zolfaghari, spokesman for the military's central command, Khatam Al-Anbiya.

items.summary: Composed at Vadtal in Gujarat in 1826 by Sahajananda Swami — Lord Swarminarayan, the Shikshapatri — or "a letter of teachings" — is devised as a guide to ethical and spiritual living

items.summary: Jose Luis Abalos and his former adviser Koldo Garcia are suspected of having pocketed kickbacks for handing out public contracts worth millions of euros for sanitary equipment during the COVID-19 pandemic

items.title: A long war in West Asia could take away support from Ukraine, says Zelenskyy
items.summary: Ukraine desperately needs more U.S.-made Patriot air defence systems to help it counter Russia’s daily barrages, Mr. Zelenskyy said, speaking to The Associated Press in an interview on April 4, in Istanbul.

items.title: For Trump, the cost of Iran war is rising
items.summary: At least six KC-135s were damaged in two Iranian missile and drone attacks at the Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia; The U.S. has also lost at least 12 MQ-9 Reaper drones since the war began, according to some reports

items.title: Southern California wildfire mostly contained as officials lift many evacuation orders
items.summary: Encompassing roughly 16 square kilometres in Riverside County, about 103 kilometres east of Los Angeles, the Springs Fire was no longer growing and at least 75% contained Saturday (April 4), said Terra Fernandez, public safety information specialist for the Riverside County Fire Department.

items.title: Iran war starts pinching U.S.; gas prices, transport costs surge

items.title: Kuwait power, water desalination plants damaged by Iranian attack: government
items.summary: Iran’s army said it was targeting “aluminum industries” in the UAE and U.S. military infrastructure in Kuwait, the official IRNA news agency reported

items.title: Iran FM Araghchi warns of contamination risk after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Bushehr nuclear power plant
items.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that continued attacks on the plant on Iran's southern coast could eventually lead to radioactive fallout that would "end life in GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) capitals, not Tehran"; Bushehr is considerably closer to Kuwait, Bahrain and Qatar than it is to the Iranian capital.

items.title: Iran-Israel war updates: Trump says Iran military leaders killed in Tehran strike
items.summary: "Many of Iran's Military Leaders, who have led them poorly and unwisely, are terminated, along with much else, with this massive strike in Tehran!" U.S. President Donald Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform.

items.summary: The Democratic congressman, who combines points from America First nationalism with the welfarism of the Democratic left, proposes state support for the development of manufacturing and the working class, and calls for moderation in maximalist tech capitalism
